verdict_reasoning: TMED9 is a type I transmembrane protein that transits through the secretory pathway including post-Golgi vesicles and reaches the plasma membrane in the context of unconventional protein secretion (UPS) under ER stress. Under baseline conditions, TMED9 is predominantly localized to the ER and ERGIC. Its steady-state accessibility on the plasma membrane outer leaflet is limited; surface display occurs primarily under stress conditions (ARF1-Q71L blocks ER-to-Golgi, triggering UPS). Cell-state_induced surface display under stress qualifies as contextual, not baseline yes.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether TMED9 maintains a small steady-state pool on the plasma membrane under baseline conditions, or only surfaces upon ER stress. Literature emphasizes ER/ERGIC residence as primary; stress-induced UPS is documented but conditional.
